habitat: Forests; gardens; plantations; mainly in highlands and montane areas
story: In the diverse world of birds, the Sri Lanka White Eye (Zosterops ceylonensis) stands out. This species, part of the Zosteropidae family, is a resident of Forests; gardens; plantations; mainly in highlands and montane areas. Its IUCN Red List status is Least Concern (LC), which serves as a call to action for habitat preservation. The Sri Lanka White Eye is more than just a bird; it is a symbol of the rich biodiversity that we must protect. Through this poster, we hope to foster a greater understanding and appreciation for the Sri Lanka White Eye and its role in the ecosystem.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
